title: MAX FPS: Optimized
description:

body:
THX 250К DOWNLOADS!!!!

![Sq](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/cc2a0a7669e61049ba4e49a7023c14a06068b48c_0.webp)

## 🔥 Why Choose My Modpack?  

**Tired of "optimization" packs bloated with useless mods?**  
My modpack contains **only 16 FPS-boosting mods** and essential libraries — **NOTHING EXTRA**. No annoying additions, just pure performance! I also actively support all versions, including old ones with major updates.

---

## 📥 Install on ANY LAUNCHER

1. Download the `.mrpack` from Modrinth
2. Convert it to `.zip` using:
   [Converter 1](https://jamie.codeberg.page/mrpack-to-zip) *or*
   [Converter 2](https://fabulously-optimized.github.io/mrpack-to-zip)
3. Install the required loader in your launcher (**Fabric / Forge / NeoForge / Quilt**)
4. Open the `.zip` archive (which you downloaded earlier) and copy everything inside (`mods`, `config`, `options.txt`) into your created instance folder
 
🎥 **Guides**

* **Legacy Launcher (old TLauncher)**
  [YouTube link](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wj-Pre8PvMk&t=57s)

* **TLauncher**
  [YouTube link](https://youtu.be/1cCFiQxEs5I)

* **KLauncher**
[RuTube link](https://rutube.ru/video/343e3a43aec3efad84c9a66c07c0d8df/?utm_source=embed&utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=main_logo&utm_content=343e3a43aec3efad84c9a66c07c0d8df&utm_term=yastatic.net&t=0)

If your launcher is not listed, find a way to **install mods/modpacks** for it yourself on **YouTube** or ask **ChatGPT**.

⭐ **Recommended**

The easiest and most convenient way is using the **Modrinth Launcher** — highly recommended!


---

## ⚡ Key Features  
- **🚀 Max FPS** — See tests in [Gallary](https://modrinth.com/modpack/max-fps-optimized/gallery)
- 🧹 **Zero bloat** — Optimization mods only  
- 📦 Lightweight + frequent updates 
- 💻 Works with all launchers (TL, Klauncher, Prism, etc.)  
- 🌐 Stable in single/multiplayer  
- 💡 Configs pre-optimized for max FPS!  

---

## 🚀 Recommended Versions  
**Fabric 1.21.1** — Best performance and updates.  

---

## ❓ FAQ  


<details>
<summary>Fabric or Quilt?</summary>
They're nearly identical, but since all mods are made for Fabric first, you'll have zero issues with Fabric. Quilt support is discontinued due to low usage and minimal benefits. I strongly recommend Fabric.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Will the Vulcan mod be added to your modpack?
</summary>
When Minecraft officially switches to Vulkan, I’ll definitely be happy and will support a build based on the Vulkan API.

Since the tests published on this page, a lot has changed, and I can confidently say that my build performs worse on OpenGL than on Vulkan (in some cases, the performance gain is nearly 3×). However, this is not true in all scenarios: many optimizations are simply missing in the Vulkan mod, which means that in heavy scenes the performance is often identical.

Therefore, at the moment I don’t see much point in using the Vulkan mod — the game will switch to the Vulkan API soon anyway, and with the current Vulkan mod most mods don’t work, and not a single modpack is fully compatible. And again, with the Vulkan mod there are no analogues to Voxy/distant horions, which allow you to see hundreds of chunks.


</details>

<details>
<summary>Will the Nvidium mod be added to your modpack?</summary>
- Never. I think this mod is extremely useless, because it only works on Nvidia video cards (AMD and Intel are out of luck), and only on the Turing architecture (GTX 16+ or RTX). That is, it only works on powerful video cards. And the weakest of them - 1650 is already quite powerful, and without shaders it will have very high fps. And you want and have the opportunity to play on it with shaders. Playing on RTX video cards without shaders is pointless. The mod itself is only available for Fabric 1.19.4+, for which there are almost no heavy mod builds for playing on GTX 1650 without shaders at low FPS. This is just an overpraised mod. Instead, I would recommend you Distant Horizons

</details>

<details>
<summary>Will you develop your modpack on CurseForge or somewhere else?</summary>
Most likely, no. I will definitely never develop my modpack on CurseForge and abandoned it because of the stupid moderation system that does not allow adding mods from other sites, requires a moderator to check every action and imposes a bunch of other useless restrictions. I do not want to spend so much time reworking my modpack for CurseForge, because users end up downloading mods separately. This is stupid. Starting with version 3.0 of my modpack, anyone can download it and copy it to any launcher, even without a Minecraft license

</details>

<details>
<summary>Are there/will there be mods that everyone needs (like iris), but do not affect optimization?</summary>
No and never will be. The main feature of my modpack is the absence of mods that do not affect performance. The user can decide for himself whether he needs these mods and download them if necessary. In general, I recommend downloading mods from my modpack and adding them to your assemblies. </details>
